title: Dumas Beach
tagline: Dumas Beach — beach town of Gujarat

overview.short: Dumas Beach is a rural beach along the Arabian Sea, located 21 kilometres (13 mi) southwest of the city of Surat in the Indian state of Gujarat.
overview.description: Dumas Beach is a rural beach along the Arabian Sea, located 21 kilometres (13 mi) southwest of the city of Surat in the Indian state of Gujarat. It is a popular tourist destination in South Gujarat. It is said to be haunted and was a burial site.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Pithwala Stadium
topPlaces.description: Pithawala Stadium is a cricket stadium in Surat, Gujarat, India. The stadium has two Ranji Trophy matches in 1989 and 1992.

topPlaces.name: Mindhola River
topPlaces.description: Mindhola River is a river in western India in Gujarat whose origin is near Doswada, Songadh. Its basin has a maximum length of 105 km (65 mi).

topPlaces.name: Tapti River
topPlaces.description: The Tapti River (or Tapi) is a river in central India located south of the Narmada River that flows westward before draining into the Arabian Sea. The river is approximately 724 km (450 mi) and flows through the states of Maharashtra, Gujarat and Madhya Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Battle of Surat (1704)
topPlaces.description: The Battle of Surat took place on 2 February 1704, off Surat, India, Arabian Sea, when a Portuguese squadron of one ship of the line and seven frigates, commanded by D. António de Meneses, defeated an Omani squadron of nine ships of the line and frigates.

topPlaces.name: Vipul shipyard
topPlaces.description: The Vipul shipyard is a shipbuilding works at Magdalla Port in the Surat district of Gujarat. As of 2007, the works consisted of a slipway and 100 meters of waterfront spread across 8 acres of land.

topPlaces.name: Vesu
topPlaces.description: Vesu is a suburban area located in South West Zone, of Surat. Vesu is the newest area to develop in terms of public transport infrastructure, residential complexes, business parks and shopping arcades.

topPlaces.name: Hazira Port
topPlaces.description: Hazira Port or Surat Port is a deep-water liquefied natural gas (LNG) terminal and multi-cargo deep-water port about 20 miles southwest of Surat, India built by Essar Group. It was originally designed as a captive port for Essar Steel.

topPlaces.name: Hazira Gas Processing Complex
topPlaces.description: Hazira Gas Processing Complex (also known as HGPC) is a natural gas processing plant operated by Oil and Natural Gas Corporation in Hazira, India.

items.note: Pithawala Stadium is a cricket stadium in Surat, Gujarat, India. The stadium has two Ranji Trophy matches in 1989 and 1992.
Mindhola River is a river in western India in Gujarat whose origin is near Doswada, Songadh. Its basin has a maximum length of 105 km (65 mi).

The Tapti River (or Tapi) is a river in central India located south of the Narmada River that flows westward before draining into the Arabian Sea. The river is approximately 724 km (450 mi) and flows through the states of Maharashtra, Gujarat and Madhya Pradesh.

The Battle of Surat took place on 2 February 1704, off Surat, India, Arabian Sea, when a Portuguese squadron of one ship of the line and seven frigates, commanded by D. António de Meneses, defeated an Omani squadron of nine ships of the line and frigates.
The Vipul shipyard is a shipbuilding works at Magdalla Port in the Surat district of Gujarat. As of 2007, the works consisted of a slipway and 100 meters of waterfront spread across 8 acres of land.

Vesu is a suburban area located in South West Zone, of Surat. Vesu is the newest area to develop in terms of public transport infrastructure, residential complexes, business parks and shopping arcades.

Hazira Port or Surat Port is a deep-water liquefied natural gas (LNG) terminal and multi-cargo deep-water port about 20 miles southwest of Surat, India built by Essar Group. It was originally designed as a captive port for Essar Steel.
Hazira Gas Processing Complex (also known as HGPC) is a natural gas processing plant operated by Oil and Natural Gas Corporation in Hazira, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Dumas Beach?
faq.a: The best time to visit Dumas Beach is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Dumas Beach?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Surat Airport (5 km) and the nearest railway station is Surat Railway Station (19 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Dumas Beach?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Dumas Beach at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Dumas Beach?
faq.a: Stays in Dumas Beach start from ₹1,200 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Dumas Beach?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pithwala Stadium, Mindhola River, Tapti River, Battle of Surat (1704), Vipul shipyard.
